suggested_topics: the engineering of ancient Roman aqueducts
how mycelium networks share resources underground
the economics of food trucks in major cities
the role of smell in human memory formation

formats.name: microscope_view
formats.instruction: Zoom in on a single moment, object, or detail you love or loathe. Describe it so vividly the reader could reconstruct it — lavish textures, smells, shapes. Limit to 25-40 words.

formats.name: future_letter
formats.instruction: Write a short letter to your future self — 1-2 years from now — about your hopes, worries, and what you wish you'll remember. Let your personality shine through. 120-200 words.

formats.name: devil_advocate_flash
formats.instruction: Take a commonly accepted belief and argue the opposite. Only ONE aggressive paragraph, maximum 75 words, minimum 25 characters. Don't hedge or apologize.

formats.name: soundtrack_scene
formats.instruction: Describe a real-life event (mundane or dramatic) as if it’s a scene in a film: include a soundtrack, camera angles, mood lighting. Put the reader in the director’s chair. 130-230 words.

formats.name: poetic_blueprint
formats.instruction: Explain a practical process or skill step-by-step, but write each step as a vivid metaphor or poetic image. Mix helpfulness and beauty. 60-120 words.

formats.name: 3_worst_mistakes
formats.instruction: List the three most disastrous mistakes you’ve made in a specific area. For each, describe what happened and one lesson learned. Keep each mistake to a sentence. 45-90 words total.

formats.name: rant_in_haiku
formats.instruction: Express your irritation about a topic in exactly three haikus. No explanations — just emotion in poetic form. 15-17 words per haiku.

formats.name: open_invitation
formats.instruction: Invite readers to an imaginary event that solves a problem or uplifts a community. Describe the venue, vibe, and who’s welcome. Add a twist that makes it unique. 130-250 words.

formats.name: reverse_recipe
formats.instruction: Describe a finished product, experience, or feeling, then ask readers to guess how it might have been 'cooked up'. No instructions, just clues. One tight paragraph, 40-80 words.

formats.name: whiplash_list
formats.instruction: Compose a rapid-fire list of 10 wildly contrasting opinions, feelings, or predictions about a topic, alternating positive/negative. No explanations—just the list. 30-55 words.

title_styles: Write a title as a question that might spark a heated debate, e.g., 'Can nostalgia actually make us worse at innovation?'
Write a title using vivid imagery, e.g., 'Why I think making mistakes is the gasoline of creativity', 'This is what happens when your world turns upside-down'
Compose a title that teases an unlikely connection, e.g., 'What gardening taught me about online arguments', 'How my broken toaster unraveled a tech mystery'
Write a title that promises a strange confession or secret, e.g., 'Confession: I wish every city was a museum', 'Secretly, I enjoy being misunderstood'
Write a title that invites the reader into an imaginary experience, e.g., 'Welcome to the event where anxiety gets a makeover', 'Imagine a world where deadlines bring joy'
structure_variants: Write as a dueling dialogue — two personas with opposing views arguing back and forth, each getting equal space.
Open with an unexpected metaphor, then unravel the topic through it.
Use countdown structure: start at 5 and reveal stories, facts, or ideas in reverse order.
Structure as a letter from one fictional character to another about the real-world topic.
Start with a silent moment or pause, then let the story or idea build gradually.
Write with abrupt scene changes, like a montage, jumping between snapshots that converge at the end.
topic_angles: The hidden joys and anxieties of waiting — airports, lines, anticipation
What happens behind the scenes at mundane workplaces (grocery stores, post offices, repair shops)
The impacts of micro-decisions: tiny choices that ripple outward
Why failure can be stylish, and how embracing it creates culture
Objects with secret stories — everyday items harboring unexpected histories
